places.name: Emirates DXB → Zürich (ZRH) + van pickup
places.famousFor: Direct ~6.5 h, Economy open-jaw (return from Vienna). Collect 9-seat van + boosters at ZRH.
places.goodToKnow: Book 7 seats together; IDP for each driver

places.name: Baur au Lac (or Park Hyatt Zurich)
places.near: City centre near the lake and Paradeplatz; ~10 min walk from Zürich HB down Bahnhofstrasse, or tram 2/6/7/8/9/11/13 to Paradeplatz then ~4 min walk.
places.famousFor: Lakeside garden icon steps from Bahnhofstrasse — private, discreet, family suites
places.goodToKnow: Book 2 family rooms early; valet garage ~CHF 55/night

places.name: Bahnhofstrasse
places.order: Window-shop luxury brands, watches and jewellery, visit Swiss chocolate shops, walk down to Lake Zurich at the end
places.famousFor: Zurich's premier luxury shopping boulevard running from the main station to Lake Zurich, one of the world's most exclusive and expensive shopping streets
places.goodToKnow: Tram-served, largely traffic-calmed; high-end. Festive lights in winter

places.name: Lindenhof
places.near: ~7-min walk from Zürich HB along the Limmat, then up. Easiest via the eastern staircase (~60 steps) from the river, or a gentler approach up Schlüsselgasse from St. Peter. Tram 6/7/11/13 to Rennweg, then a short walk.
places.order: Free to visit any time. Take in the viewpoint over the river and rooftops, relax under the lindens, and watch locals play the oversized outdoor chess games (near knee-high pieces). Great sunset spot.
places.famousFor: A tranquil linden-shaded hilltop square — site of a 4th-century Roman fort and later a Carolingian palace — offering one of the best free panoramas over the Old Town, the Limmat, Grossmünster and the university.
places.goodToKnow: Open 24/7, no ticket. Go early morning or golden hour for photos and fewer crowds. Family- and stroller-friendly if you take the ramped/gentler routes rather than the staircase; benches and shade make it a good rest stop.

places.name: Augustinergasse
places.near: Old Town (Altstadt), off Bahnhofstrasse near St. Peter
places.order: Small independent shops, Swiss souvenirs and handmade crafts; admire the historic facades
places.famousFor: Charming medieval Old Town lane with colorful houses and carved wooden oriel windows
places.goodToKnow: Quiet, picturesque pedestrian lane; quick to walk; great for photos

places.name: Fraumünster Church
places.area: Altstadt (Old Town), left bank at Münsterhof
places.near: Tram 4 or 15 to Helmhaus, or walk ~7 min from Zürich HB down Bahnhofstrasse to Paradeplatz, then to Münsterhof. Directly across the river from the Grossmünster.
places.order: Buy the entry ticket and take the audio-guide (~45 min) covering the Chagall and Giacometti windows, the church history and Reformation, and the crypt exhibition on over 1,200 years of city history. Best light on the choir windows is on a bright day.
places.famousFor: Home to five monumental stained-glass windows (each ~10 m high) designed by Marc Chagall in 1970, plus a rose window by Chagall and a north-transept window by Augusto Giacometti — a highlight of 20th-century religious art.
places.goodToKnow: Generally open daily ~10:00–18:00; Mar–Oct closed to tourists on Sunday until after the morning service. Small admission fee. Photography of the Chagall windows is typically restricted — check on-site signage. Family-friendly; the audio-guide keeps kids engaged.

places.name: Café & Restaurant Confiserie Sprüngli — Paradeplatz
places.near: Trams 6/7/8/11/13 to Paradeplatz; ~8 min from HB down Bahnhofstrasse
places.order: Luxemburgerli (assorted box or by the piece), hot chocolate, a Truffe du Jour, seasonal cake
places.famousFor: Zurich institution since 1859; home of the iconic Luxemburgerli macarons, truffles du jour and grand café tradition
places.goodToKnow: Nostalgic 1st-floor Café & Restaurant plus ground-floor café bar; a must-do classic; busy — go off-peak; family-friendly

places.name: Bürkliplatz
places.area: Zurich city centre (west lakeshore, at the lake outflow)
places.near: Trams 2, 5, 8, 9 and 11 stop at Bürkliplatz; central and walkable from Zurich HB (~15 min) via Bahnhofstrasse.
places.order: Walk the promenade for the Alps view, catch a ZSG lake cruise from here, browse the Saturday flea market (May–October, 230+ stalls) or the twice-weekly 'Bürklimäärt' vegetable market.
places.famousFor: The central lakefront square where Lake Zurich meets the city, offering the classic view down the lake to the Alps on clear days, plus the main boat terminal and a famous flea market.
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round; go on a clear day (or at sunset) for the best Alps-over-the-lake photos. Flea market is seasonal (Saturdays, May–October). Family-friendly; combine with a boat trip or the Bahnhofstrasse walk.

places.name: Haus Hiltl
places.near: About a 7–10 minute walk from Zurich HB south down Bahnhofstrasse, then a short turn onto Sihlstrasse; or tram 6, 7, 11, or 13 to Rennweg/Paradeplatz area, then a short walk. Corner building near Bahnhofstrasse.
places.order: The legendary Hiltl buffet (weigh-and-pay, 100+ hot and cold dishes — mushroom stroganoff, Thai and Indian curries, Middle Eastern mezze), plus à la carte veg classics; there is also Switzerland's first vegetarian butchery on site.
places.famousFor: The world's oldest vegetarian restaurant (founded 1898), recognized by Guinness World Records; family-run into its fourth generation. Famous for its huge pay-by-weight buffet of 100+ international veg and vegan dishes.
places.goodToKnow: Open long hours daily (breakfast through late evening); the buffet is priced by weight, ideal for flexible portions and mixed groups. Very family- and vegan-friendly with clear allergy/vegan labelling. Reservations recommended for the served restaurant at peak lunch/dinner; the buffet and takeaway are walk-in. Also houses the Hiltl Academy (cooking classes) and a club upstairs.

places.name: Lindt Home of Chocolate
places.area: Kilchberg (Lake Zurich shore, ~7 km south of Zurich)
places.near: From Zurich Hauptbahnhof take S-Bahn S8 or S24 (~15 min) to Kilchberg station, then a short walk or Bus 165/163 to Schokoladenplatz. Note Kilchberg is outside Zurich fare zone 110, add zone 150. By car ~15 min via the Thalwil-Rüschlikon exit; underground parking on site.
places.order: Book the timed 'Chocolate museum' single ticket for the self-guided multimedia tour ending at the giant fountain with free tastings; optionally add a hands-on chocolate-making course. Leave time for the huge Lindt shop and the café.
places.famousFor: Interactive Swiss chocolate museum at Lindt's home site, famous for the world's largest chocolate fountain (about 9 m tall, ~1,500 kg of flowing chocolate), plus the biggest Lindt shop (500 m²) and tastings.
places.goodToKnow: Very popular, book a timed slot online in advance and arrive within your 30-minute window. Allow ~1.5-2 hours for the tour plus shop. Kid-friendly and fully indoor (ideal on a rainy day). The fountain is the best photo spot. Tastings included with museum entry.

places.name: ZSG-Schifffahrt
places.near: Board at Bürkliplatz landing stage in the city centre; trams 2, 5, 8, 9, 11 stop at Bürkliplatz. Also boardable at Zürichhorn and other lakeside stages. ZVV/Swiss Travel Pass often valid on scheduled routes.
places.order: Take the short 'Kleine Rundfahrt' (~1h25) round trip to Zürichhorn and back (approx. CHF 9.40 in 2nd class), or the long 4h15 crossing to Rapperswil and back (approx. CHF 28 in 2nd class); themed cruises include the Breakfast/Late-Riser's Brunch boat, Fondue Cruise and Steamboat Dinner.
places.famousFor: Scenic scheduled boat cruises across Lake Zurich with views of the shoreline villas, hills and, on clear days, the Alps; the classic Zurich lake experience with over 1.5 million passengers a year.
places.goodToKnow: Summer timetable runs 3 April–18 October 2026 with peak-season (most departures) 1 May–30 September 2026; more limited service in winter. Tickets at the Bürkliplatz office or on board. Family-friendly; sit on the upper open deck for the best photos and Alps views. Book themed dinner/brunch cruises in advance.

places.name: Fischerstube Zürihorn
places.near: Tram 2 or 4 to Fröhlichstrasse, then a short walk to the lake; or bus 912/916 from Bellevue to Chinagarten; also reachable by lake ship to Zürichhorn. About 10-15 min from Zurich HB by tram.
places.order: Fischknusperli (crispy fried fish) with fries; Fisch-Tagesfang (daily fresh catch); goat cheese in filo pastry; cheese fondue in winter; Zurich classics.
places.famousFor: A restaurant-village built on stilts over Lake Zurich with a 128-seat panorama terrace facing the lake, skyline, mountains and sunset; fish straight from Lake Zurich and nearby waters.
places.goodToKnow: Book the Seeterrasse ahead in summer and for sunset - it is the prime spot. The complex also has a self-service Fischergarten, a take-away kiosk and an event hut, so it works for both a sit-down meal and a casual lakeside bite. Open roughly 11:30-23:30 daily. Great for lake/sunset photos.

places.name: Zürichhorn
places.near: Tram 2 or 4 to Höschgasse or Fröhlichstrasse, or ZSG lake boat to the Zürichhorn landing stage; also a pleasant ~30-min walk along the lake from Bürkliplatz.
places.order: Stroll the promenade from Bürkliplatz to Zürichhorn, watch Tinguely's 'Heureka' kinetic sculpture run (daily at 11:00, 15:00 and 19:00 for ~8 min in season), picnic on the lawns, and visit the adjacent Chinese Garden; a lake boat also stops here.
places.famousFor: A relaxed lakeside park at the end of the eastern promenade, with landscaped meadows, sculptures (Jean Tinguely's kinetic 'Heureka', Henry Moore, Pavillon Le Corbusier nearby), swimming spots and the Chinese Garden.
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round; liveliest and greenest May–September. Family-friendly with playgrounds and lakeside eateries. 'Heureka' operates in the warmer season; time your visit to one of the three daily run times for the moving sculpture.

places.name: Hotel UTO KULM car-free hideaway in Zurich
places.near: S10 S-Bahn from Zurich HB to Uetliberg (endpoint, ~20 min, every 30 min). NOTE: due to SZU tunnel construction the underground tracks 21/22 at Zurich HB are closed 29 April–18 October 2026, so the S10 runs only from Selnau (an 8-min walk from HB, or tram to Stauffacher).
places.order: Ride the S10 to the top, walk ~10 min to the summit, then climb the lookout tower (small fee ~CHF 2) for the 360° view; optionally hike the scenic Planet Trail (Planetenweg) ridge path toward Felsenegg (~2 hrs).
places.famousFor: Zurich's 'house mountain' (869 m) with a panoramic observation tower giving sweeping views over the city, the whole length of Lake Zurich and the Alps on clear days.
places.goodToKnow: Go on a clear day for Alps views; sunset and autumn/winter 'above the fog' inversion days are spectacular. Family- and stroller-friendly path from the station. Uto Kulm hotel/restaurant with a large view terrace at the summit.

places.name: Coco Grill & Bar
places.near: Right at Paradeplatz - trams 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 13 to Paradeplatz stop; about a 10-minute walk (or one tram stop) south from Zurich HB.
places.order: Charcoal-grilled Angus fillet, aged bone-in cote de boeuf, veal cutlets, rib-eye, or Tomahawk; from the sea: rock lobster, monkfish, European sea bass (loup de mer); market-fresh vegetables and sides. Pair with the wine list.
places.famousFor: Instagrammable glam grill house on Paradeplatz with a leafy Mediterranean herb-garden courtyard tucked into Zurich's banking district; charcoal grill is the centerpiece and the room is a see-and-be-seen spot.
places.goodToKnow: Upper price segment (expect roughly CHF 150-200+ per person with wine). Book ahead, especially for a courtyard-garden table in warm months, which is the most romantic/photogenic seating. Smart-casual to elegant dress fits the crowd. Open kitchen and glowing grill make for great photos; evenings are lively.

places.name: Zoo Zürich
places.near: Zurichbergstrasse 221; tram 6 from the main station to Zoo terminus
places.order: The immersive Masoala Rainforest hall and the elephant park
places.famousFor: Zoo Zurich, famous for its Masoala Rainforest and Kaeng Krachan elephant park
places.goodToKnow: Open daily year-round, from 9:00 (closes earlier in winter).

places.name: Drive Zürich → Grindelwald
places.famousFor: ~1 h 30 · easy motorway then scenic valley climb via Interlaken
places.goodToKnow: Swiss e-vignette required

places.name: Bergwelt Grindelwald — Alpine Design Resort
places.near: At the Bergwelt resort in the village (Bergwelt 4), short walk/drive from the centre
places.famousFor: Modern design resort with pool & spa under the Eiger north face
places.goodToKnow: Eiger-view family rooms; Terrassenweg alternative for quieter stay

places.name: Grindelwald-First (Top of Adventure)
places.near: Six-seater gondola from the First station in Grindelwald village (Dorfstrasse), ~25 min ride with a mid-station at Bort.
places.order: Gondola return ticket (approx. CHF 76 May–Oct, CHF 72 off-season); pair with the Adventure Package (CHF ~69 for one activity up to ~CHF 99 for all four).
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's flagship adventure summit with a cluster of thrill activities and jaw-dropping Eiger, Mönch and Wetterhorn panoramas — the region's most Instagrammed mountain playground.
places.goodToKnow: Adventure activities (Flyer, Glider, Cart, Trottibike) run roughly mid-May to end of October, weather permitting; gondola runs year-round. Free travel with Jungfrau Travel Pass, 50% off with Swiss Travel Pass / Half Fare Card / Berner Oberland Pass.

places.name: First Cliff Walk by Tissot
places.near: Walk directly from the First top station; the entrance is right beside the exit.
places.order: Included free with your Grindelwald-First gondola ticket — no separate fee. Go early morning or late afternoon for empty-railing photos.
places.notes: 15:00 Cliff walk — free, queue for photo
places.famousFor: A metal walkway bolted to the sheer First cliff face, leading to a suspension footbridge and a viewing platform that juts 45 m out over the void — one of the most photogenic spots in the Alps.
places.goodToKnow: Typically accessible ~08:30–17:30 in season; can close in high wind, snow or ice. Free once you hold a gondola ticket.

places.name: Bort Alpine Playground
places.near: Grindelwald-First gondola, alight at the Bort mid-station.
places.order: Free to play; access with your Grindelwald-First gondola ticket and get off at Bort mid-station. Restaurant and snack kiosk on site.
places.famousFor: A 700 m² natural adventure playground under the Eiger, centred on an 8.5 m tall climbable wooden cow, with a dammable mountain stream, marmot- and squirrel-themed play, rope nets, balancing elements and outdoor bowling.
places.goodToKnow: Best in summer; snow-covered in winter. A natural break point when Trottibiking down from First.

places.name: EigerBean – The Coffee Roastery
places.cuisine: Third-wave specialty coffee; pastries; coffee beans & gifts
places.near: Dorfstrasse 195, on the main street about a 2-minute walk from the First gondola station and easy from Grindelwald station.
places.order: A pour-over or espresso from the house roast, cold brew with Swiss tonic, the 'Coffee Bon-Bon' (espresso dessert drink), or cascara (coffee-cherry) tea; grab a bag of beans to take home.
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's dedicated specialty-coffee spot, founded 2023 by roaster Marco Münch. Beans roasted in-house (sail-shipped green coffee via Belco, three roast profiles), local Lenk dairy milk, window seats with a mountain outlook.
places.goodToKnow: Small, modern, roastery-style space; typically open daily ~9:00–17:00. Pastries are sourced from a nearby bakery rather than baked on-site.

places.name: Barry's Restaurant, Bar & Lounge (Eiger Mountain & Soul Resort / Hotel Eiger)
places.near: Village centre on Dorfstrasse, a few minutes' walk from Grindelwald Terminal/train station
places.order: Raclette, cheese fondue and other Swiss classics; seasonal international mains
places.famousFor: One of the most popular tables in the village (4.3 stars, 1,700+ reviews); cosy romantic niches and reliable Swiss classics
places.goodToKnow: Wide-appeal menu good for families and couples alike; busy in season, so reserve. Has a bar/lounge but the restaurant itself is dining-focused

places.name: Eiger Express Tricable Gondola
places.near: Departs from Grindelwald Terminal (Grundstrasse 54), directly reachable by train and with large car parking.
places.order: Ride it as the fast leg of a Jungfraujoch round trip, or ride up to Eigergletscher just for the Eiger-face views and the Eiger Trail.
places.famousFor: A state-of-the-art tricable gondola gliding beneath the notorious Eiger North Face, reaching Eigergletscher in ~15 minutes and cutting the trip to the Top of Europe by ~47 minutes versus the old route.
places.goodToKnow: Out of service for maintenance 20–24 April 2026 and 9–20 November 2026; seat reservation required on the onward Jungfraujoch train May–Oct (children also from CHF 10).

places.name: Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe
places.near: Eiger Express from Grindelwald Terminal to Eigergletscher, then the Jungfrau Railway; ~45 minutes door-to-summit.
places.order: Full Jungfraujoch round-trip ticket (via Eiger Express up + Jungfrau Railway); allow at least half a day. Ticket includes Sphinx, Ice Palace and Alpine Sensation.
places.famousFor: Europe's highest railway station, set on a glacier saddle between the Mönch and Jungfrau, with the Sphinx observation terrace, the Ice Palace carved into the Aletsch Glacier, the Alpine Sensation walk, and views over the Aletsch — the longest glacier in the Alps (UNESCO).
places.goodToKnow: Seat reservation mandatory May–October; open 365 days but weather can flatten the view — check the summit webcam before committing. Dress warmly (Ice Palace is ~−3 °C year-round); altitude can affect some visitors.
places.address: Jungfraujoch station, 3801 Fieschertal / accessed via Grindelwald Terminal, Switzerland

places.name: Wengernalpbahn (Grindelwald ↔ Kleine Scheidegg Cog Railway)
places.near: Board at Grindelwald / Grindelwald Terminal (Grund).
places.order: Ride Grindelwald–Kleine Scheidegg (~24 min) for the Eiger-base views; combine with the Männlichen Panorama Trail or continue to Jungfraujoch.
places.notes: 14:30 Scenic cog-rail loop via Kleine Scheidegg
places.famousFor: The world's longest continuous cog railway, its panorama trains climbing past alpine meadows to Kleine Scheidegg (2,061 m) at the very foot of the Eiger — a scenic ride in its own right and the connector to the Panorama Trail and Jungfraujoch.
places.goodToKnow: Runs year-round with half-hourly panorama trains; the Grindelwald–Kleine Scheidegg line closes for maintenance 26 October–6 November 2026.

places.name: Ringgenberg Bäckerei & Tea Room
places.cuisine: Swiss bakery, cakes, pastries, light lunch
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village centre, walkable from the station
places.order: Fresh pastries, cakes and tarts; coffee and a light lunch
places.famousFor: Long-standing village bakery-café known for fresh breads, cakes and tarts
places.goodToKnow: Great family/kid-friendly breakfast and coffee-and-cake stop; not a dinner venue

places.name: Golden India
places.near: In the village, walkable from the centre
places.order: Curries, tandoori dishes and fresh naan
places.famousFor: Grindelwald's go-to Indian kitchen — authentic curries, tandoori and naan; a popular change of pace from Swiss fare
places.goodToKnow: Good vegetarian selection; family-friendly. Verify exact address/hours locally

places.name: Männlichen Cableway & Royal Walk
places.near: Grindelwald–Männlichen gondola (GGM) from Grindelwald Terminal, or the Royal Ride cableway from Wengen.
places.order: Gondola up from Grindelwald Terminal (~19 min), do the ~25-min Royal Walk, then walk the Panorama Trail to Kleine Scheidegg and train back down.
places.famousFor: A panoramic ridge between the Grindelwald and Lauterbrunnen valleys; the short 'Royal Walk' climbs to the summit crown for a 360° sweep of the Eiger, Mönch, Jungfrau and Tschuggen.
places.goodToKnow: Summer gondola/cableway season ~23 May–25 October 2026. Summer offer: up to 50% off public transport to Männlichen plus 10% off the Wengen cable car, 1 July–31 Aug 2026.
places.address: Männlichenbahn, Grindelwald Terminal, Grundstrasse 54, 3818 Grindelwald, Switzerland

places.name: Lieselotte Themed Family Trail (Männlichen → Holenstein)
places.near: Gondola to Männlichen, then walk the marked Lieselotte-Weg down to Holenstein.
places.order: Free to walk with your Männlichen gondola ticket; ~600 m descent, about 1.5 hours to the Holenstein mid-station.
places.notes: 10:30 Kids' themed trail down to Holenstein
places.famousFor: A storybook downhill trail following the cartoon cow Lieselotte, with 13 interactive stations (refresh the chickens, play a real alphorn, collect letters for a solution word) beneath the Eiger North Face.
places.goodToKnow: Not stroller-suitable; easiest walked June–October. Combine with the Sennenspielplatz playground (with the 8.5 m wooden Lieselotte) at the top.

places.name: Pfingstegg Cable Car
places.near: Pfingstegg valley station is a short walk/bus from Grindelwald village centre.
places.order: Round-trip cable car; add the FunPackage combining the toboggan run and Fly-Line. Free travel with Berner Oberland Pass.
places.famousFor: A quick, short cable car to a sunny terrace facing the Eiger, Mättenberg and the twin Grindelwald glaciers — the base for the toboggan run, Fly-Line and quieter high-trails.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 summer season starts 25 April; mountain restaurant opens 9 May. Facility can close at short notice in poor weather.

places.name: Pfingstegg Summer Toboggan Run (Rodelbahn)
places.near: Take the Pfingstegg cable car up; the toboggan run is right at the top station.
places.order: Buy rides on site (single or multi-ride); no advance reservation possible. Bundle with the Fly-Line in the FunPackage.
places.famousFor: A 736 m rail-guided summer toboggan track dropping 58 m with speeds up to 40 km/h, banking through curves with the glaciers as a backdrop — you control your own brake.
places.goodToKnow: Cable car opens 25 April 2026 but the toboggan run typically opens later (around 22 May 2026) depending on snow/weather; closed in rain or wet conditions for safety.

places.name: Grindelwald Glacier Gorge (Gletscherschlucht)
places.near: Short bus (toward Grindelwald Gletscherschlucht) or a pleasant walk from the village centre.
places.order: Entry ticket for the gorge walk; brave the Spiderweb net stretched ~7 m above the rushing water. Berner Oberland Pass gives a discount.
places.famousFor: Walkways and tunnels threaded between 300 m marble-streaked rock walls along the roaring Lütschine, with themed sections on geology, water and myths — and the thrilling 170 m² 'Spiderweb' net suspended over the gorge.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal — generally open late spring to autumn; closed in winter. Cool and damp inside; light jacket recommended.

places.name: Onkel Tom's Hütte (Onkel Tom's Pizza & Wine)
places.cuisine: Wood-fired pizza (also rösti/fondue) + large wine list
places.near: On Dorfstrasse in the village, walkable from the centre
places.order: Wood-oven pizza; pair with a bottle from the extensive list; rösti and fondue also available
places.famousFor: Tiny, atmospheric wooden ski-hut room with 400+ wines and beloved pizzas; a Grindelwald institution
places.goodToKnow: Very small and popular — reservations strongly recommended; cosy for couples but works for families too

places.name: Trümmelbach Falls (Lauterbrunnen day trip)
places.near: From Grindelwald, train to Zweilütschinen then Lauterbrunnen (~37 min), then Bus 141 (~10 min) to the Trümmelbachfälle stop.
places.order: Entry ticket; ride the internal funicular up, then walk down past all 10 falls. Pair with the free-falling Staubbach Falls in Lauterbrunnen village.
places.famousFor: Europe's largest subterranean waterfalls — ten glacier-fed falls thundering inside the mountain, reached via a tunnel lift and galleries — in the UNESCO Lauterbrunnen 'Valley of 72 Waterfalls'.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~09:00–17:00 April–November (08:30–18:00 in July/August); closed in winter. Cold, wet and loud inside — bring a light jacket.

places.name: Drive Grindelwald → Chamonix
places.famousFor: ~3 h via Spiez · Montreux · Martigny — lakes first, high alpine last
places.goodToKnow: Fuel up in Switzerland

places.name: Hôtel Mont-Blanc Chamonix
places.near: Central Chamonix, off Allée du Majestic; walkable from anywhere in town.
places.famousFor: 5★ feel in the pedestrian centre — walk to Aiguille du Midi cable car
places.goodToKnow: Ask for Mont Blanc-view room + parking

places.name: Chamonix Town Centre & Église Saint-Michel
places.near: The heart of Chamonix; walkable from the train station and all central hotels.
places.order: A leisurely stroll around Place Balmat and Rue du Docteur Paccard; photograph the church and statues; browse mountaineering boutiques and patisseries.
places.famousFor: A postcard-perfect Alpine town of pastel façades, flower-lined balconies and café terraces along the Arve, crowned by the Église Saint-Michel and the pioneering Balmat-Saussure and Dr Paccard statues pointing to Mont Blanc.
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round. Come early morning or near sunset for the best light on the peaks behind town and quieter streets. The riverside and old bridges are especially photogenic.

places.name: Chalet 4810
places.near: Central Chamonix; walkable.
places.order: Hot chocolate, pastries and cakes; light savoury plates for a mid-day break.
places.famousFor: A very pretty, stylish tea room — one of the most Instagrammable spots in town — for coffee, chocolate and delicate sweet and savoury bites.
places.goodToKnow: Great daytime/family stop and a photo spot; not a dinner restaurant. Named after Mont Blanc's height (4,810 m).
places.address: Chalet 4810, 191, Rue Joseph Vallot, Les Plans, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: Le Cap Horn
places.cuisine: Modern French, seafood & sushi with Asian accents
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue des Moulins; take the stairs up from street level to the upper floors.
places.order: Fresh seafood platters, the sushi menu, and the memorable desserts; extensive wine list.
places.famousFor: Elegant, nautical-themed dining above one of Chamonix's oldest streets; seafood-forward menu and a notably popular sushi selection.
places.goodToKnow: Warm wooden interior, elegant atmosphere; higher-end mains; reservations recommended. Good for couples or a special dinner; ranked ~#26 on Tripadvisor with ~1,980 reviews.

places.name: Aiguille du Midi Cable Car & Step into the Void
places.near: Departs from the Aiguille du Midi station a short walk south of Chamonix town centre; well signed, paid parking and bus stops nearby.
places.order: Round-trip adult ticket to the 3,842 m summit (approx. 83 EUR); the panoramic terrace elevator and Step into the Void are free but require an on-site timed reservation. Book a morning slot online in advance for clear skies and to guarantee your cable-car time.
places.notes: 08:00 BOOK 1st cars — 3,842 m, Step into the Void
places.famousFor: The Chamonix icon: a vertigo-inducing cable car soaring from town to 3,842 m for face-to-face Mont Blanc panoramas, plus the glass 'Step into the Void' skywalk box suspended over a 1,000 m drop.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season roughly 13 Jun-13 Sep (extended summer hours Jul-Aug from ~6:10 am). Altitude 3,842 m means cold, thin air and possible altitude effects even in summer - bring warm layers. Closes in poor visibility; reservations mandatory for the skywalk elevator.
places.address: 100 Place de l'Aiguille du Midi, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Refuge du Plan de l'Aiguille
places.area: Plan de l'Aiguille (~2,207 m), Aiguille du Midi mid-station
places.near: Aiguille du Midi cable car to the Plan de l'Aiguille mid-station, then ~10–20 min walk; also reachable on foot via the Grand Balcon Nord.
places.order: Tarte aux myrtilles and the other fruit tarts (raspberry, lemon-kiwi, pear); hearty homemade alpine dishes and soup.
places.famousFor: A rustic sun-trap terrace with huge views over the Aiguilles Rouges and the valley — and arguably the best tarte aux myrtilles (blueberry tart) in Chamonix (run by a former pâtissier).
places.goodToKnow: Summer/hiking season; cash-friendly mountain hut vibe; a fantastic family or couples lunch stop mid-hike; warm hospitality noted in reviews.

places.name: Le Panoramique (Montenvers – Mer de Glace)
places.area: Montenvers (1,913 m), above the Mer de Glace glacier
places.near: Montenvers rack railway (red train) from Chamonix (~20 min, 1,035 m → 1,913 m); restaurant is at the arrival station.
places.order: Slow-cooked traditional French dishes; go for a leisurely lunch with the glacier view.
places.notes: 14:30 Rack rail to Mer de Glace
places.famousFor: A light-filled dining room and vast terrace facing the Drus and the Mer de Glace glacier, reached by the historic little red Montenvers train. Spectacular, family-friendly setting.
places.goodToKnow: Combine with the Mer de Glace ice cave and train ride (great for families); reserve for lunch; requires train ticket. Also on-site: the simpler Refuge du Montenvers restaurant.

places.name: Grotte de Glace (Mer de Glace Ice Cave)
places.near: Reached via the Montenvers train, then a short gondola down toward the glacier, then a staircase.
places.order: Included with the Montenvers round-trip ticket; take the small gondola down from Montenvers, then descend the metal staircase (~500+ steps round trip) to the cave.
places.famousFor: A tunnel and rooms carved by hand into the living glacier each year, lit in blue and dotted with ice sculptures - you literally walk inside the Mer de Glace.
places.goodToKnow: Open with the Montenvers season (~Jun-Nov 2026); the many steps make it a workout for the ice cave. Poignant markers show how far the glacier has retreated - a striking, Instagrammable and educational stop. Same July industrial-action caveat as the train.
places.address: Mer de Glace, below Montenvers, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Aux Petits Gourmands
places.area: Chamonix Centre (Rue du Docteur Paccard); second café in Chamonix Sud
places.cuisine: French pastry, viennoiserie, chocolate, tea & coffee
places.near: Main shop on Rue du Docteur Paccard in the town centre; smaller café in Chamonix Sud
places.order: Chocolate or coffee éclair, raspberry/berry tartelette, viennoiserie, hot chocolate; enjoy on the sunny terrace
places.famousFor: Long-standing local favourite with jewel-like pastries at fair prices and handmade chocolates, including the '4810' mini-Mont-Blanc chocolates
places.goodToKnow: Main shop open daily ~07:30-19:30; Chamonix Sud branch has seasonal hours. Beautiful window displays; family-friendly.

places.name: Casa Valerio
places.near: Central Chamonix; walkable.
places.order: Wood-fired pizzas (big menu), fresh pasta dishes; Italian wines.
places.famousFor: A long-running, genuinely Italian address in Chamonix known for a huge choice of around 50 pizzas plus fresh pasta — a dependable crowd-pleaser for families.
places.goodToKnow: Family-friendly and generous; popular with locals; reservations helpful at peak times.

places.name: QC Terme Chamonix Mont-Blanc (Luxury Spa)
places.near: On the Promenade du Fori in Chamonix (Les Pèlerins area), a short drive or bus from the centre; parking available.
places.order: Full-day or evening 'access' ticket (includes pools, saunas, steam rooms and relaxation areas); add a massage or treatment. Book in advance and aim for off-peak times.
places.famousFor: An indulgent multi-sensory spa with indoor and outdoor thermal pools, saunas, steam rooms and waterfalls, all framed by Mont Blanc views - the valley's premier luxury wellness escape.
places.goodToKnow: Open year-round; can get crowded and noisy at peak times, so go early or on a weekday for a calmer, more romantic experience. Bring swimwear; robe/towel packages available. Adults-oriented (age limits apply) - better for couples than young kids.

places.name: Drive Chamonix → Lake Como (Mont Blanc Tunnel)
places.famousFor: ~3 h 15 · tunnel toll ≈ €51 · Aosta lunch stop · last 30 min narrow lake road
places.goodToKnow: Passports handy at FR→IT

places.name: Grand Hotel Tremezzo
places.near: Tremezzo, Lake Como; ~1 hr from Milan airports
places.famousFor: Iconic 1910 palace with floating pool — heart of the ferry triangle
places.goodToKnow: Book lake-view family suite far ahead — 1 night only

places.name: Villa del Balbianello
places.near: On the Lavedo promontory near Lenno; reach by boat from Lenno or a lakeside walk.
places.order: The arched loggia and the manicured terrace framing the lake on both sides from the point.
places.famousFor: Elegant lakeside villa on a wooded promontory with terraced loggia gardens jutting into Lake Como (a Star Wars and James Bond filming site).
places.goodToKnow: Ticketed, with separate garden and villa tours; check open days. Arrive by water taxi for the classic approach.

places.name: Bellagio Old Town & Salita Serbelloni
places.near: Steps up directly from Bellagio's ferry pier and main lakefront.
places.order: Wander the salite and lakefront, climb Salita Serbelloni for the classic shot, and browse Como silk and artisan boutiques.
places.famousFor: The postcard heart of the lake: steep cobbled 'salite' (stepped alleys), the iconic Salita Serbelloni lined with colorful buildings, silk shops and boutiques cascading to the water.
places.goodToKnow: Free to explore; allow 2–3 hours. Very crowded midday in summer — early morning or evening is best for photos and calm. Cobbles and stairs are steep (limited stroller/wheelchair access).

places.name: Gelateria del Borgo
places.near: On Via Garibaldi in Bellagio's picturesque old town, up the salita (stepped streets) a short climb from the waterfront.
places.order: The pistachio (repeatedly singled out as exceptional), plus vegan chocolate, mint and lemon sorbets.
places.famousFor: Widely called the best gelato in Bellagio - artisanal, made with natural ingredients, tucked along the charming stepped old-town lanes.
places.goodToKnow: Reasonable prices (a few euros for two to three scoops). Can get busy; the setting on Bellagio's photogenic staircase streets is a highlight.

places.name: Al Veluu
places.near: On Via IV Novembre above Tremezzo; best by car (steep climb from the lakefront).
places.order: Traditional regional dishes; a terrace table at lunch or sunset for the sweeping view.
places.famousFor: A hillside restaurant on the slopes above Tremezzo with an unrivaled panorama over Lenno, Isola Comacina and Villa Balbianello.
places.goodToKnow: One of the best views in Tremezzina; reserve a view table. Romantic; drive or taxi up. (Seasonal - typically closed in winter.)

places.name: Varenna Village & Passeggiata degli Innamorati
places.near: Ferry to Varenna from Bellagio/Menaggio; the walkway links the pier to the village center.
places.order: Stroll the Passeggiata degli Innamorati at golden hour, then wander the stepped lanes and lakefront piazza.
places.famousFor: A quieter, deeply romantic fishing village of pastel houses, with the 'Walk of Lovers' — a lakeside boardwalk suspended over the water connecting the ferry area to the old town.
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round; the boardwalk can close briefly in high water/storms. Pairs naturally with Villa Monastero, Vezio Castle and Fiumelatte.
places.address: Passeggiata degli Innamorati, 23829 Varenna LC, Italy

places.name: Villa Monastero
places.near: Varenna, Lake Como (Lecco province); ferry to Varenna or train then short walk
places.order: Walk the lakefront terraces; tour the period house-museum and citrus collection
places.famousFor: Lakeside villa-museum on Lake Como, a former Cistercian convent, with a ~2 km terraced botanical garden of palms, agaves, citrus and roses
places.goodToKnow: Garden opened 1940; site hosts an international physics conference center

places.name: Drive Lake Como → Bolzano
places.famousFor: ~3 h 30 · A4 + A22 via Verona · Sirmione (Lake Garda) lunch stop
places.goodToKnow: Italian autostrada tolls — pay card

places.name: Parkhotel Laurin
places.near: In the Parkhotel Laurin, Via Laurin, a few minutes' walk from the station and Piazza Walther
places.famousFor: Art-nouveau grande dame with garden, steps from the arcades
places.goodToKnow: Get hotel's ZTL entry route + garage instructions (verify)

places.name: Piazza Walther (Waltherplatz)
places.near: Heart of the Old Town, directly beside the Cathedral; 5-10 min walk from the train station.
places.order: Free to stroll; best at golden hour with the Duomo's tiled roof behind. Host square of the Christmas Market in winter.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's grand main square (laid out in 1808), ringed by cafés and pastel façades, with the white-marble monument to medieval minstrel Walther von der Vogelweide at its center. The city's living room and prime people-watching / Instagram spot.
places.goodToKnow: Open public space, always accessible and free. Very lively in summer and during the Christmas Market (late Nov-early Jan). No caveats.

places.name: Zur Kaiserkron
places.cuisine: Contemporary Mediterranean with South Tyrolean touches
places.near: Central old town on Piazza della Mostra, a short walk from Piazza Walther; easy on foot
places.order: Seasonal tasting menu; signature plates such as fig-and-veal-sweetbread risotto, roast venison; ask about the gourmet vs. casual offering
places.famousFor: Elegant vaulted dining rooms in historic Palazzo Pock; refined seasonal degustation cuisine by chef Filippo Sinisgalli; one of Bolzano's top special-occasion tables
places.goodToKnow: Two concepts under one roof (Casual + Gourmet); extensive local/Italian wine list. Closed Sun and Mon; lunch and dinner Tue-Sat. Reservations recommended

places.name: South Tyrol Museum of Archaeology (Ötzi the Iceman)
places.near: Central Old Town, a 5-minute walk from Piazza Walther via Via Museo / near the Talvera river.
places.order: Standard admission with an audioguide; reserve a timed entry slot online to skip queues. Allow 1.5-2 hours.
places.famousFor: Home of Ötzi, the 5,300-year-old naturally mummified 'Iceman' found in the Ötztal Alps in 1991 — one of the world's most important archaeological finds, viewable through a small refrigerated window kept at -6°C and 98% humidity.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue-Sun 10:00-18:00 (also open Mondays in July & August); closed Jan 1, May 1, Dec 25. Adults ~€13, family (2 adults + children under 16) ~€26, under 6 free. Advance online reservation strongly recommended in high season. Verify 2026 hours/prices on the official site.

places.name: Piazza delle Erbe Market (Obstmarkt / Fruit Market)
places.near: In the Old Town between Via dei Portici and the Talvera side; 3-min walk from Piazza Walther.
places.order: Graze on local speck, alpine cheeses, fresh pretzels, seasonal fruit; roasted chestnuts in autumn. Great for edible souvenirs.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's daily open-air market, running since 1295 — colourful stalls of South Tyrolean fruit, vegetables, bread, flowers, speck, cheese and spices, anchored by the 1749 Neptune Fountain. A feast for the senses.
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon-Fri 07:00-19:00, Sat 07:00-13:00; CLOSED SUNDAYS. Year-round. Free to browse.

places.name: Via dei Portici (Laubengasse) Arcades
places.near: Runs between Piazza delle Erbe and the Town Hall Square in the Old Town.
places.order: Window-shop and café-hop under the arcades; duck into the narrow deep 'Laubenhäuser' courtyards. A classic Instagram corridor.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's oldest and most beautiful street — a 300 m run of 12th-century arcaded merchant houses with Gothic arches, oriel windows and wrought-iron signs, lined with elegant boutiques, cafés and traditional shops.
places.goodToKnow: Public street, always accessible; shops typically Mon-Sat with a common midday break at some smaller stores. Busy on Saturdays.

places.name: Renon/Ritten Cable Car (Rittner Seilbahn / Funivia del Renon)
places.near: Valley station on Via Renon, a ~10-min walk from Bolzano train station.
places.order: Round-trip ticket; gondolas depart roughly every 4 minutes. Combine with the historic Renon narrow-gauge railway at the top. Prams and bikes welcome; children under 6 travel free.
places.famousFor: A modern panoramic gondola that floats from central Bolzano up to the sunny Ritten (Renon) high plateau at 1,227 m in just ~12 minutes, with sweeping views over the city and Dolomites — one of the easiest 'up into the mountains in minutes' experiences in the Alps.
places.goodToKnow: Runs year-round with high frequency; brief annual maintenance windows possible — check before travel. Buy tickets at the station or via South Tyrol mobility.

places.name: Renon/Ritten Narrow-Gauge Railway (Rittnerbahn)
places.near: Board directly across from the cable-car top station in Soprabolzano/Oberbozen.
places.order: Ride the full line Oberbozen–Klobenstein; combine with the cable car up from Bolzano for a classic half-day loop. Alight at Collalbo to walk to the Longomoso earth pyramids.
places.famousFor: The last narrow-gauge railway in South Tyrol (opened 1907), running partly with charming historic wooden carriages across the Ritten plateau with postcard Dolomite views the whole way.
places.goodToKnow: Operates year-round on a regular timetable; historic-carriage runs are on select departures. Children under 6 ride free. Check current timetable.

places.name: Earth Pyramids of Ritten/Renon
places.area: Ritten plateau (Katzenbach below Soprabolzano; Longomoso near Collalbo)
places.near: Via the Renon cable car from Bolzano, then the Rittnerbahn railway and a short marked hiking trail.
places.order: Easy family option: take the railway to Collalbo/Klobenstein and walk trail 24 to the Longomoso pyramids (~30 min each way); or from Soprabolzano follow the 'sentiero piramidi' via trail 23 to the Katzenbach viewpoint.
places.famousFor: Surreal, needle-like clay 'earth pyramids' each topped by a balancing boulder — said to be Europe's tallest and most perfectly formed, carved from glacial moraine over ~25,000 years. Otherworldly and highly Instagrammable.
places.goodToKnow: Free, open-air natural monument. Best in dry weather (clay trails are slippery when wet). Wear proper shoes; viewpoints are fenced. Accessible spring-autumn; winter snow may limit access.

places.name: Laboratorio Espresso (Tropical Caffè)
places.area: Don Bosco / Via Milano (west of center)
places.near: About a 15-20 min walk west of Piazza Walther along Via Milano; easy by bus or car (residential area, not the tourist core).
places.order: A single-origin flight tasted three ways (espresso, French press, paper filter); traceable single-origin espresso; V60 pour-over.
places.famousFor: Bolzano's original specialty-coffee address; run by coffee-obsessed Luca Pintimalli. Family business since 1981 (on the site of Tropical Caffè, open since 1959), renovated 2020.
places.goodToKnow: The real deal for coffee nerds and a genuine hidden gem - not a scenic old-town terrace but the best cup in town. Beans from artisan micro-roasteries across Italy/Europe are sold to take home. Check opening days (closed Sun; limited weekend hours).

places.name: Wirtshaus Vögele
places.near: Via Goethe 3, in the heart of the old town just off Piazza Erbe; on foot
places.order: Vögele's Knödel-Kistel (dumpling sampler), homemade Schlutzkrapfen with spinach-cheese filling, speck, and the best Kaiserschmarren in town
places.famousFor: Beloved historic inn in a Biedermeier-style building, home to 'the oldest regulars' table in South Tyrol'; a must-visit institution for traditional dishes
places.goodToKnow: Spread over several floors with cozy wood-panelled stube rooms; family- and visitor-friendly, open from morning coffee to dinner. Closed Sun; reservations recommended

places.name: Drive Bolzano → Innsbruck (Brenner Pass)
places.famousFor: ~1 h 45 · the easiest scenic Alpine crossing · Sterzing coffee stop
places.goodToKnow: Buy Austrian digital vignette + Brenner toll BEFORE entering

places.name: Swarovski Kristallwelten (Crystal Worlds)
places.near: In Wattens, ~20 km east of Innsbruck; direct shuttle from Innsbruck city centre, regional bus, or car (large car park).
places.order: Explore the Chambers of Wonder, walk under the shimmering Crystal Cloud in the garden, let kids loose on the huge play tower and outdoor playground, and browse the flagship crystal store.
places.famousFor: A famous crystal-themed art and experience park built into a hillside behind the Giant's head fountain — surreal 'Chambers of Wonder' art installations, a giant crystal Cloud and mirrored Crystal Dome.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 9:00-19:00, last entry 18:00 (verify 2026; holiday hours may differ). Outdoor areas best in fair weather.

places.name: Daniels Kristallwelten by DoN
places.near: At Swarovski Kristallwelten in Wattens, ~15 km east of Innsbruck; large visitor parking
places.order: Daily lunch specials and à la carte; in-house patisserie (macarons, cakes); weekend brunch
places.famousFor: A design-forward café-restaurant (Snøhetta interior with crystal-infused surfaces) in Swarovski Crystal Worlds' 'Garden of the Giant' – accessible without a museum ticket.
places.goodToKnow: Daytime venue (brunch/lunch, à la carte to ~17:30); great for families visiting the attraction; verify 2026 hours

places.name: Hotel Innsbruck (Altstadt) — or similar 5★
places.famousFor: One-night old-town base — Golden Roof, cafés & Nordkette on foot
places.goodToKnow: Needs verification — confirm family rooms & parking

places.name: Goldenes Dachl (Golden Roof) & Museum
places.near: In the pedestrian heart of Innsbruck Old Town (Altstadt); walk from the main station (~10 min) or any city centre point. No parking in the old town — use nearby city garages.
places.order: Photograph the gilded oriel from the Old Town square, then visit the small museum inside on Maximilian I and the roof's history; wander the surrounding medieval alleys and Maria-Theresien-Straße.
places.famousFor: Innsbruck's most iconic landmark: a late-Gothic oriel balcony roofed with 2,657 gilded copper shingles, built for Emperor Maximilian I around 1497-1500.
places.goodToKnow: Roof itself viewable free anytime. Museum hours (verify 2026): May-Sep daily 10:00-17:00; Oct-Apr Tue-Sun 10:00-17:00, Mon closed.

places.name: Nordkette Cable Car (Hungerburgbahn to Hafelekar)
places.near: Board the Hungerburgbahn at the Congress valley station (Rennweg 3) in the city centre; connect via Seegrube to Hafelekar.
places.order: Ride the Hungerburgbahn funicular then the two Nordkettenbahnen gondolas up to Seegrube and Hafelekar for panoramic views over Innsbruck and the Karwendel; easy ridgeline walks at the top.
places.famousFor: A Zaha Hadid-designed funicular plus gondolas whisk you from the city centre to the Hafelekar summit (2,334 m) in about 25 minutes — city-to-alpine-wilderness in one ride.
places.goodToKnow: Operates year-round but sections close for maintenance in spring/autumn and in high winds — check daily operating status before going. Top is cold/windy even in summer; bring a layer.

places.name: Café Konditorei Munding (seit 1803)
places.near: Kiebachgasse 16 in the old town, just off the Golden Roof
places.order: Innsbrucker Torte; a praline and a Wiener Melange
places.famousFor: Tyrol's oldest confectionery-café (since 1803), 100 m west of the Golden Roof in the historic Gumpp house; some tortes still baked to inherited family recipes, pralines from Valrhona.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue–Sat approx. 09:00–18:00; closed Sun & Mon. Verify 2026.

places.name: Gasthof Weisses Rössl
places.near: In Innsbruck's Old Town on Kiebachgasse, a minute from the Golden Roof
places.order: Tiroler Gröstl, beef tartare from Tyrolean Alpine ox, game from the house hunt, Kaiserschmarrn
places.famousFor: A beloved historic inn serving Tyrolean cuisine since 1590, steps from the Golden Roof; cozy wood-panelled parlours and a genuine local following.
places.goodToKnow: Mon–Sat lunch 11:30–14:00 and dinner 17:30–21:30; closed Sun/holidays; reservations recommended; verify 2026

places.name: Drive Innsbruck → Vienna
places.famousFor: ~4 h 45 · A12 → A1 · Mondsee lake stop · longest leg — leave by 9:00
places.goodToKnow: Two driver breaks; downloads for kids

places.name: Hotel Sacher Wien — or similar 5★
places.famousFor: Vienna's legendary address behind the Opera — cathedral & Golden U on foot
places.goodToKnow: Car goes to garage until the airport; connecting rooms early

places.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz (directly beneath the cathedral).
places.order: Climb the 343 steps of the South Tower for city views, take the lift up the North Tower to the Pummerin bell, and visit the catacombs.
places.famousFor: Vienna's Gothic landmark cathedral with its zigzag-tiled roof, 136 m 'Steffl' south tower and the massive Pummerin bell.
places.goodToKnow: Nave open Mon–Sat ~09:00–11:30 & 13:00–16:30, Sun/holidays 13:00–16:30; tower/catacomb tickets separate. Hours shift for services. Verify 2026.

places.name: Demel
places.near: On Kohlmarkt, a short walk from the Hofburg
places.order: Anna Torte, Sachertorte (Demel version), candied violets, Annatorte and hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Imperial-era pastry shop and former k.u.k. court confectioner (since 1786); ornate interior and display kitchen
places.goodToKnow: Premium prices; watch confectioners at work; busy with tourists

places.name: NENI am Naschmarkt
places.near: U4 Kettenbrückengasse, right at the Naschmarkt
places.order: Hummus; sabich; the 'Balagan' mezze breakfast/spread; shakshuka; lamb kebab
places.famousFor: The buzzy market-side flagship of the NENI brand, serving vibrant, shareable Middle-Eastern-Mediterranean plates in an energetic Naschmarkt setting.
places.goodToKnow: Great for sharing/families; terrace popular. Reservation advised at peak times. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Schönbrunn Palace & Gardens
places.near: U4 to Schönbrunn or Hietzing, then a short walk; trams 10/60 to Schönbrunn.
places.order: Grand Tour of the state apartments with audioguide; then wander the free Baroque gardens up to the Neptune Fountain, the maze, and the Privy Garden.
places.famousFor: The Habsburgs' 1,441-room imperial summer residence and Vienna's most-visited attraction, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home of Maria Theresa and a young Emperor Franz Joseph.
places.goodToKnow: Palace open daily from 09:30 (closing 16:00–18:30 seasonally); timed entry — book online in advance as slots sell out. Gardens free, gates open ~06:30. Verify 2026 hours online.

places.name: Gloriette & Café Gloriette (Schönbrunn)
places.near: Within Schönbrunn Park; U4 Hietzing then uphill through the gardens.
places.order: Walk up (10–15 min) for the panorama of the palace and Vienna skyline; the ground-floor café serves imperial breakfast, or pay for the rooftop viewing terrace.
places.famousFor: The 1775 arcaded hilltop pavilion crowning the Schönbrunn gardens, built to celebrate Habsburg power — one of the best budget viewpoints in Vienna.
places.goodToKnow: Café open daily ~09:00–20:00; the paid roof terrace runs seasonally (approx. Apr–early Nov), last entry 30 min before close. Verify 2026 terrace dates.

places.name: Tiergarten Schönbrunn (Vienna Zoo)
places.near: U4 to Hietzing, ~10 min walk; main entrance near Hietzinger Tor.
places.order: See the giant pandas (returned May 2025), koalas, orangutans, elephants and Siberian tigers; visit the historic Baroque central pavilion.
places.famousFor: Founded in 1752, the world's oldest continuously operating zoo, set within the Schönbrunn palace park with over 500 species including giant pandas.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily from 09:00; closing varies by month (16:30 winter to 18:30 summer). Adults ~€27, kids 6–18 ~€15.50, under 6 free. Verify 2026.

places.name: Austrian National Library — State Hall (Prunksaal)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; entrance on Josefsplatz.
places.order: Wander the towering book-lined hall, admire the ceiling frescoes and antique globes — a top Instagram spot inside the Hofburg complex.
places.famousFor: An 80 m-long Baroque library hall of 1723 with a frescoed dome and ~200,000 historic books — one of the most beautiful libraries in the world.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00–18:00 (Thu to 21:00); Oct–May closed Mondays. Under-19s free. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Wiener Prater & Riesenrad (Giant Ferris Wheel)
places.near: U1/U2 Praterstern, right by the entrance.
places.order: Ride the ~65 m Riesenrad for city views; enjoy the amusement rides in the Wurstelprater, or cycle/stroll the green Prater.
places.famousFor: Vienna's historic public park and Wurstelprater funfair, crowned by the 1897 Giant Ferris Wheel — a symbol of the city (featured in 'The Third Man').
places.goodToKnow: Ferris wheel open year-round (roughly 09:00–23:45, seasonal). Individual funfair rides mostly operate ~Mar–Oct/late autumn and are pay-per-ride. Verify 2026 season.

places.name: Café Sacher Wien
places.order: Original Sacher-Torte with unsweetened whipped cream, Wiener Melange, hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Home of the Original Sacher-Torte inside Hotel Sacher, behind the State Opera
places.goodToKnow: Expect queues; dress is smart-casual; the torte is also sold boxed to take away

places.name: Plachutta Wollzeile
places.near: U3 Stubentor or U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~5–7 min walk
places.order: Tafelspitz served in its copper pot with the classic accompaniments (rösti, apple-horseradish, chive sauce); Wiener Schnitzel
places.famousFor: The definitive address for Tafelspitz (boiled beef in broth), where Viennese families gather for milestones. A polished, quintessentially Viennese dining experience.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential (1–2 weeks ahead). Open daily; verify 2026 hours. Portions are generous.

places.name: Emirates Vienna (VIE) → DXB
places.famousFor: Evening departure — or fly next morning for a relaxed finale (verify best timing)
places.goodToKnow: Return the van at VIE; allow 2.5 h before flight
